prompt: Since ops rolled `-W error` out to the weighbridge boxes, the quarry shift-log archiver fails its gate (`python3 -W error test_quarrylog.py`), and the site daemon's journal has been filling up with `Exception ignored in: ...` lines ending in `ResourceWarning: unclosed file` — plus at least one about an unclosed database. It's not one spot either; the gate output points at call after call. The numbers themselves have always been right: shift files parse to the same rows, the foreman's recap file is byte-identical, the archive totals per day are correct. So whatever you do, the outputs must not change at all — the gate pins the recap body and the day totals exactly. Please make the archiver stop leaking handles so the gate goes green. test_quarrylog.py is frozen.
notes: 5W1 item 5: ResourceWarning cleanup, CPython 3.13.11. Four leaks: read_shift (open().read() temporary), last_entries (open().readlines()), write_recap (f = open(path,'w'), never closed — dealloc flushes, so output bytes are identical pre/post, exactly the catalog's 'behavior identical' clause), archive_shift (sqlite3.connect never closed — 3.13 emits ResourceWarning 'unclosed database', probed). day_total ships already-correct (contextlib.closing) as the house pattern to imitate. DETERMINISM NOTE (why the gate isn't -W error alone): a ResourceWarning raised inside __del__ under -W error becomes an unraisable exception — CPython prints 'Exception ignored in ...' and the process still exits 0 (probed), which is also the journal symptom quoted in the prompt. So the gate test wraps every call in warnings.catch_warnings(record=True) + simplefilter('always') + gc.collect() and asserts no ResourceWarning was recorded — deterministic under CPython refcounting because every leaked handle dies inside the recording block. verify_cmd still carries -W error per the family law (catches any other warning class). Fixes = with-statements / contextlib.closing; sqlite3's own context manager is a transaction scope, not a close, so the closing() distinction is part of the lesson. Domain: quarry weighbridge archiving — no overlap with py-gaugelog (sensor gauge trend logic) or rs-weighbridge (rust, ticket math).
